title: Object-Oriented Architecture and Capstone Delivery · Program Forensics
summary: Deliver an extensible community-resource workflow with a domain core, replaceable boundaries, migrations, and operator evidence. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a production-shaped object-oriented capstone and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and e…
objectives: Separate domain rules, application orchestration, infrastructure adapters, and user interface concerns with explicit dependencies.
Use domain services only for cohesive rules that do not naturally belong to one entity or value object.
Version persisted representations and migrate data without coupling domain objects directly to historical storage schemas.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
